methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $87M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Osaro, a Series B robotics and manufacturing automation company founded by Derik Pridmore and Michael Kahane, has raised $86.8M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 9 private robotics firms at the same stage plus 1 public comparator, establishing a reference set with meaningful heterogeneity in scale.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised ($87M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $165M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same-stage companies—including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M)—yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B.

The triangulated valuation range is $140M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $620M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression ($165M) and peer-median ($1.1B) reflects material uncertainty around whether Osaro's competitive position, technology maturity, and market traction align with higher-valued peers or represent a discount to median. Key uncertainties include revenue scale, customer concentration, deployment success rates in field robotics, and the trajectory of manufacturing automation adoption. The peer set itself exhibits substantial valuation variance, limiting confidence in any point estimate without additional operational metrics.
